subject: Gaming
title: Nintendo's War on Fan Projects
description: One-minute skill: Nintendo's War on Fan Projects.

hook.text: Nintendo shuts down fan games, ROM sites, and YouTube videos. It's the most aggressive IP enforcer in gaming.

buildup.text: Fan projects like AM2R (a Metroid remake) and Pokemon Uranium were hit with takedowns despite being free.

discovery.text: Nintendo argues that fan projects damage their brand and IP value. Legally, they're usually correct.

twist.text: Other companies embrace fan creations. Sega's official response to fan Sonic games was to hire the creators.

climax.text: Nintendo's approach is legally sound but culturally costly. Fans who love their games feel punished for it.

punchline.text: Fans made games out of love. Nintendo sent lawyers. Love met litigation.

quiz.question: How does Nintendo typically respond to fan-made game projects?
options: They aggressively issue takedown notices and legal action
They encourage and support fan projects
